messages.author: zera777
messages.content: @Elyot How did you determine the unit supply numbers? 1, 4, 10, 20 seems at least a little arbitrary. I would do powers of 2, 3, or 4. Also, I know that P1 can build an extra drone because some players complained, but what if drones had infinite supply instead?

threads.formatted_text:
--- Thread (22 messages, 2019-03-24 to 2019-03-25) ---
[2019-03-24 23:36] elyot: @Zera I have no idea how we came up with 1/4/10/20, I think we just started with 10 and realized we needed smaller and larger numbers for some units.
[2019-03-24 23:38] elyot: @ruinedshadows Yeah we've always thought about that, doing raids or even a single player version of them. I think both are a lot of work, both from a design and from a production standpoint. We'd almost rather make another game based on those topics, or a spinoff.
[2019-03-24 23:41] elyot: @Deleted User It's hard to say, like if we had hired 1 more full-time person for a year or two then we might be done by now, but we might also be kinda broke by now. Also more people don't always make things faster. A lot of the founding team never really got paid, like we're almost all investors in the game itself so it wouldn't make much sense to invest money in the game and then just pay it back to ourselves and get taxed. The rule has always been that founders can work on the game as much or as little as they want (and get company shares according to the hours they put in) and some people are literally profs now so they're very busy
[2019-03-24 23:43] elyot: Like I'm hardcore into prismata and game dev because running my own game developer/publisher is really what I wanna do, but not everyone on the founding team is like that, some want to work on their own stuff or make cash immediately or pursue an academic career and prismata is more of a side project to them or something that they only did full time for a few years
[2019-03-24 23:44] elyot: even some of the paid staff we hired have moved onto other things, or stuff like the AI or the art basically got to a satisfactory level so there wasn't much more for them to do
[2019-03-24 23:46] elyot: so there are a lot of tasks that really only a handful of us are really capable of doing, and in a lot of cases I'm the only one among those capable who is putting in substantial hours, though I do try to give as much of the programming and even basic design work to some of the junior folks who are working for us
[2019-03-24 23:54] zera777 (Zera): @Elyot Since you are no doubt a gamer of impeccable taste, what are your 10 favorite games made by other people?

[2019-03-25 00:20] zera777 (Zera): That's an odd choice, considering the kind of game you made.
But I guess varied taste is essential to a designer.
[2019-03-25 00:20] elyot: nah prismata is clearly just turn-based brood war 😛

[2019-05-03 00:11] elyot: @Drevoed Actually we did think of that. We might do it. threads.formatted_text:
--- Thread (5 messages, 2019-10-08) ---
[2019-10-08 10:43] elyot: @Drevoed 
1) Q never clicks anything with a cost to click, that's just how it was implemented
2) The Tia example seems like a bug, can you send me a replay code in a bug report in game? The Mahar/Gauss Cannon example is intended behaviour, basically because Mahar is a higher-priority target (it doesn't take healing into account)
3) We've heard that suggestion before and it seems like a reasonable idea (you could QQ to click basically everything clickable on the table). We just never really found an implementation that we loved, and it's never been a high enough priority to really dive in deep to problem-solve around the design. But it's certainly doable.
[2019-10-08 10:44] elyot: Also yes, I want to add more emotes, suggestions are welcome.
[2019-10-08 10:45] elyot: And regarding the spectating indicator, I think we were just too lazy to add it. Right now, the client doesn't know whether there are spectators (only the server does), so it's somewhat non-trivial to add (but honestly not that hard)
[2019-10-08 10:50] drevoed: Tia Breach on Turn 8: dKp4p-59qyW Can't send in game rn.
[2019-10-08 10:55] elyot: it's fine, I'll just send it myself ^_^

messages.author: elyot
messages.content:
@Drevoed 
1) Q never clicks anything with a cost to click, that's just how it was implemented
2) The Tia example seems like a bug, can you send me a replay code in a bug report in game? The Mahar/Gauss Cannon example is intended behaviour, basically because Mahar is a higher-priority target (it doesn't take healing into account)
3) We've heard that suggestion before and it seems like a reasonable idea (you could QQ to click basically everything clickable on the table). We just never really found an implementation that we loved, and it's never been a high enough priority to really dive in deep to problem-solve around the design. But it's certainly doable.

messages.author: elyot
messages.content: Also yes, I want to add more emotes, suggestions are welcome.

messages.author: elyot
messages.content: And regarding the spectating indicator, I think we were just too lazy to add it. Right now, the client doesn't know whether there are spectators (only the server does), so it's somewhat non-trivial to add (but honestly not that hard)
